drugs.notes: Administered as intravenous bolus in 50ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 10 minutes. Dose banded as per CSCCN agreed bands. Vinorelbine is a vesicant — observe extravasation precautions.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils less than 1.5x10⁹/L on day of treatment
dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment for 7 days in the first instance. If counts recover, continue at initial dose. If counts remain low after delay, apply 20% dose reduction (factor 0.80). If myelosuppression recurs despite dose reduction, stop treatment.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils less than 1.5x10⁹/L — persistent after 7-day delay
dose_modifications.notes: Continue with 20% dose reduction. If myelosuppression recurs despite this reduction, stop treatment.

dose_modifications.condition: Platelets less than 100x10⁹/L on day of treatment
dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment for 7 days in the first instance. If counts recover, continue at initial dose. If counts remain in this range after delay, apply 20% dose reduction.

dose_modifications.condition: Platelets less than 100x10⁹/L — persistent after 7-day delay
dose_modifications.notes: Continue with 20% dose reduction.

dose_modifications.condition: Platelets less than 50x10⁹/L
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce dose by 50%.

dose_modifications.condition: Severe hepatic impairment
dose_modifications.notes: Consider dose reduction to 20mg/m² for the intravenous preparation in severe liver impairment.

dose_modifications.condition: Day 8 dose adjustment
dose_modifications.notes: Dose adjustments for day 8 should be made according to local practice guidelines or procedures.

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment (any degree)
dose_modifications.notes: No dose adjustment necessary for renal impairment.

dose_modifications.condition: Third dose reduction required
dose_modifications.notes: If a third dose reduction is necessary, stop treatment.

pre_medications: Metoclopramide 10mg oral or intravenous 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy
Metoclopramide 10mg three times a day oral when required (take home)
special_warnings: Vinorelbine is a vesicant — extravasation precautions must be observed.
Second line therapy only: indicated for stage III or IV NSCLC with WHO Performance Status 0, 1 or 2.
Minimum criteria before prescribing cycle 1 day 1: neutrophils ≥1.5x10⁹/L and platelets ≥100x10⁹/L.
Consider blood transfusion if patient is symptomatic of anaemia or has haemoglobin less than 8g/dL.
FBC, LFTs and U&Es required prior to each cycle.
Chest X-ray should be performed before each cycle.
Baseline chest X-ray and up-to-date cross-sectional imaging (ideally within 1 month) required before starting treatment.
Gastric protection with a proton pump inhibitor or H2 antagonist may be considered in patients at high risk of GI ulceration or bleed.
Prophylactic antibiotics can be considered if required.
If a third dose reduction is necessary, stop treatment.
All dose reductions due to adverse drug reactions should not be re-escalated in subsequent cycles without consultant approval.
Palliative intent.

drugs.notes: Cycle 1 only: 60mg/m² (max 120mg) on days 1, 8 and 15. Dose rounded to nearest 10mg (up if halfway). Available as 20mg, 30mg and 80mg capsules. Swallow whole with cold water — do not chew or suck. Take with food. Store in refrigerator away from foodstuffs. | CYCLE 1 DOSE (60mg/m²): two Vinorelbine entries exist — 60mg/m² for cycle 1 (initial tolerated dose) and 80mg/m² for cycles 2-4 if nadir criteria met. Both entries are intentional and represent different cycle doses.

Cycles 2, 3, 4: 80mg/m² (max 160mg) on days 1, 8 and 15, provided nadir criteria are met during the first three 60mg/m² administrations. Dose rounded to nearest 10mg (up if halfway). Available as 20mg, 30mg and 80mg capsules. Swallow whole with cold water — do not chew or suck. Take with food. Store in refrigerator away from foodstuffs. | CYCLES 2-4 DOSE (80mg/m²): two Vinorelbine entries exist — 60mg/m² for cycle 1 and 80mg/m² for cycles 2-4 if nadir criteria met. Both entries are intentional and represent different cycle doses.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophil count during first 3 administrations at 60mg/m²: more than 1x10⁹/L (all 3 doses) OR 0.5-1x10⁹/L on 1 episode only
dose_modifications.notes: Escalate to 80mg/m² from the 4th administration onwards.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophil count during first 3 administrations at 60mg/m²: 0.5-1x10⁹/L on 2 or more episodes OR less than 0.5x10⁹/L on any occasion
dose_modifications.notes: Remain at 60mg/m² from the 4th administration onwards (do not escalate to 80mg/m²).

dose_modifications.condition: At any administration planned at 80mg/m²: neutrophils below 0.5x10⁹/L OR more than once between 0.5-1x10⁹/L
dose_modifications.notes: Delay administration until recovery, then reduce dose from 80mg/m² back to 60mg/m² for the following three administrations.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophil count beyond 4th dose of 80mg/m²: 0.5-1x10⁹/L (2 episodes) OR less than 0.5x10⁹/L
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce dose to 60mg/m² for the next administration. Re-escalation from 60 to 80mg/m² is possible if neutrophil count does not drop below 0.5x10⁹/L or more than once between 0.5-1x10⁹/L during three administrations at 60mg/m², per the rules for the first three administrations.

dose_modifications.condition: Mild hepatic impairment: bilirubin less than 1.5xULN and ALT/AST 1.5-2.5xULN
dose_modifications.notes: Administer at standard dose of 60mg/m²/week.

dose_modifications.condition: Moderate hepatic impairment: bilirubin 1.5-3xULN (regardless of ALT/AST)
dose_modifications.notes: Administer at reduced dose of 50mg/m²/week.

dose_modifications.condition: Severe hepatic impairment
dose_modifications.notes: Oral vinorelbine is contra-indicated in severe hepatic impairment.

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment (any degree)
dose_modifications.notes: No dose adjustment necessary for renal impairment.

dose_modifications.condition: Peripheral neuropathy NCI-CTC grade 2
dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment for 7 days. If resolved to grade 1 or below, resume at 60mg/m² (or 50mg/m² if 60mg/m² was current dose). If not resolved within 7 days, consider stopping treatment.

dose_modifications.condition: Peripheral neuropathy NCI-CTC grade 3 or above
dose_modifications.notes: Consider stopping treatment.

dose_modifications.condition: Any other NCI-CTC grade 3 or above toxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Defer treatment for 7 days until resolved to grade 1 or below, then restart at 60mg/m² (or 50mg/m² if 60mg/m² was current dose). If more than 7 days required for resolution, consider stopping treatment.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophil and platelet counts not sustained over first three weeks
dose_modifications.notes: If neutrophil and platelet levels are not sustained over the first three weekly administrations, consider stopping treatment.

dose_modifications.condition: Third dose reduction required
dose_modifications.notes: If a third dose reduction is necessary, stop treatment.

pre_medications: Ondansetron 8mg oral taken 15-30 minutes prior to vinorelbine (take home medication)
Ondansetron 8mg oral may be taken 12 hours later if required for nausea/vomiting
Metoclopramide 10mg oral three times a day when required (consider supplying on cycle 1 for relief of nausea)
special_warnings: NPSA/2008/RRR001: The National Patient Safety Alert on oral chemotherapy MUST be followed in relation to oral vinorelbine.
This protocol is for NSCLC stage III or IV, palliative intent.
FBC once weekly for first four weeks of treatment, then three-weekly thereafter. Neutrophil and platelet count must be checked weekly for the first three weeks.
Baseline chest X-ray and up-to-date cross-sectional imaging (ideally within 1 month) required before starting treatment.
Cycle 1 dose is 60mg/m² (max 120mg). Dose escalation to 80mg/m² (max 160mg) from cycle 2 is conditional on acceptable nadir counts during the first three administrations.
Oral vinorelbine dose rounded to the nearest 10mg (up if halfway).
Vinorelbine capsules must be swallowed whole with cold water — do not chew or suck.
Take vinorelbine capsules with or after food.
Vinorelbine capsules must be stored in a refrigerator, away from foodstuffs.
Available as 20mg, 30mg and 80mg capsules.
Oral vinorelbine is contra-indicated in severe hepatic impairment.
Consider blood transfusion if patient is symptomatic of anaemia or has haemoglobin less than 8g/dL.
Gastric protection with a proton pump inhibitor or H2 antagonist may be considered in patients at high risk of GI ulceration or bleed.
All dose reductions due to adverse drug reactions should not be re-escalated without consultant approval.
If a third dose reduction is necessary, stop treatment.
This protocol may require funding approval.

drugs.notes: Cycle 1: 60mg/m² (max 120mg) on days 1 and 8. Cycle 2 day 1: 60mg/m² (max 120mg). Dose rounded to nearest 10mg (up if halfway). Available as 20mg, 30mg and 80mg capsules. Swallow whole with cold water — do not chew or suck. Take with food. Store in refrigerator away from foodstuffs. | STARTING DOSE (60mg/m²): two Vinorelbine entries exist — 60mg/m² for cycle 1 (days 1 and 8) and cycle 2 day 1, and 80mg/m² for cycle 2 day 8 onwards if nadir criteria met. Both entries are intentional and represent dose escalation.

Cycle 2 day 8 only: 80mg/m² (max 160mg), provided nadir criteria were met during first three 60mg/m² administrations. Cycles 3 and 4: 80mg/m² on days 1 and 8. Dose rounded to nearest 10mg (up if halfway). This is an unlicensed dosage schedule. | ESCALATED DOSE (80mg/m²): two Vinorelbine entries exist — 60mg/m² for initial doses and 80mg/m² for cycle 2 day 8 onwards if nadir criteria met. Both entries are intentional and represent dose escalation.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophil count below 1.5x10⁹/L and/or platelet count below 100x10⁹/L on day of treatment
dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment until recovery.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophil count during first 3 administrations at 60mg/m²: more than 1x10⁹/L (all doses) OR 0.5-1x10⁹/L on 1 episode only
dose_modifications.notes: Escalate to 80mg/m² from the 4th administration (cycle 2 day 8 onwards).

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophil count during first 3 administrations at 60mg/m²: 0.5-1x10⁹/L on 2 or more episodes OR less than 0.5x10⁹/L
dose_modifications.notes: Remain at 60mg/m² from the 4th administration (do not escalate to 80mg/m²).

dose_modifications.condition: At any administration planned at 80mg/m²: neutrophils below 0.5x10⁹/L OR more than once between 0.5-1x10⁹/L
dose_modifications.notes: Delay administration until recovery, then reduce dose from 80mg/m² back to 60mg/m² for the following three administrations.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophil count beyond 4th dose of 80mg/m²: 0.5-1x10⁹/L (2 episodes) OR less than 500 (0.5x10⁹/L)
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce dose to 60mg/m² for the next administration. Re-escalation from 60 to 80mg/m² is possible if neutrophil count does not drop below 0.5x10⁹/L or more than once between 0.5-1x10⁹/L during three administrations at 60mg/m².

dose_modifications.condition: Mild hepatic impairment: bilirubin less than 1.5xULN and ALT/AST 1.5-2.5xULN
dose_modifications.notes: Administer at standard dose of 60mg/m²/week.

dose_modifications.condition: Moderate hepatic impairment: bilirubin 1.5-3xULN (regardless of ALT/AST)
dose_modifications.notes: Administer at reduced dose of 50mg/m²/week.

dose_modifications.condition: Severe hepatic impairment
dose_modifications.notes: Oral vinorelbine is contra-indicated in severe hepatic impairment.

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment (any degree)
dose_modifications.notes: No dose adjustment necessary for renal impairment.

dose_modifications.condition: Peripheral neuropathy NCI-CTC grade 2
dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment for 7 days. If resolved to grade 1 or below, resume at 60mg/m² (or 50mg/m² if 60mg/m² was current dose). If not resolved within 7 days, consider stopping treatment.

dose_modifications.condition: Peripheral neuropathy NCI-CTC grade 3 or above
dose_modifications.notes: Consider stopping treatment.

dose_modifications.condition: Any other NCI-CTC grade 3 or above toxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Defer treatment for 7 days until resolved to grade 1 or below, then restart at 60mg/m² (or 50mg/m² if 60mg/m² was current dose). If more than 7 days required for resolution, consider stopping treatment.

dose_modifications.condition: Third dose reduction required
dose_modifications.notes: If a third dose reduction is necessary, stop treatment.

pre_medications: Ondansetron 8mg oral taken 15-30 minutes prior to vinorelbine (take home medication)
Ondansetron 8mg oral may be taken 12 hours later if required for nausea/vomiting
Metoclopramide 10mg oral three times a day when required (consider supplying on cycle 1 for relief of nausea)
special_warnings: NPSA/2008/RRR001: The National Patient Safety Alert on oral chemotherapy MUST be followed in relation to oral vinorelbine.
This is an unlicensed dosage schedule.
This protocol is for NSCLC stage III or IV, palliative intent.

FBC required prior to treatment on days 1 and 8 for cycles 1 and 2. May be reduced to 3-weekly monitoring (day 1 only) at clinician's discretion once haematological response is established and stable. LFTs and U&Es every 3 weeks.
Baseline chest X-ray and up-to-date cross-sectional imaging (ideally within 1 month) required before starting treatment.
Cycle 1: 60mg/m² on days 1 and 8 (max 120mg). Cycle 2 day 1: 60mg/m² (max 120mg); cycle 2 day 8: 80mg/m² (max 160mg) if escalation criteria met. Cycles 3 and 4: 80mg/m² on days 1 and 8 (max 160mg).
Oral vinorelbine dose rounded to the nearest 10mg (up if halfway).
Vinorelbine capsules must be swallowed whole with cold water — do not chew or suck.
Take vinorelbine capsules with or after food.
Vinorelbine capsules must be stored in a refrigerator, away from foodstuffs.
Available as 20mg, 30mg and 80mg capsules.
Oral vinorelbine is contra-indicated in severe hepatic impairment.
Consider blood transfusion if patient is symptomatic of anaemia or has haemoglobin less than 8g/dL.
Gastric protection with a proton pump inhibitor or H2 antagonist may be considered in patients at high risk of GI ulceration or bleed.
All dose reductions due to adverse drug reactions should not be re-escalated without consultant approval.
If a third dose reduction is necessary, stop treatment.
This protocol may require funding approval.
